pi_items.source: verifier.py and export_result.sh explicitly check for table 'EMPLOYEE_HISTORY_ARCHIVE'
verifier.py and export_result.sh explicitly check for mview 'MV_DEPT_TURNOVER'
setup_task.sh uses 'JOB_HISTORY' as the baseline table to calculate migrated rows
verifier.py (line 74) awards full points if partition_count >= 4
Task description and Oracle HR schema (job_history table has end_date column), although verifier only checks table partition status broadly
verifier.py explicitly checks for 'JOB_ID' and 'DEPARTMENT_ID' in the bitmap_indexes list returned by export_result.sh
export_result.sh hardcodes '/home/ga/Desktop/archive_analysis.txt' as the expected file path
setup_task.sh validates that JOB_HISTORY has >= 10 rows before starting the task (lines 18-20)

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the synthetic Oracle HR sample schema. It requires creating a range-partitioned table named EMPLOYEE_HISTORY_ARCHIVE with at least 4 partitions (based on END_DATE), migrating all rows from JOB_HISTORY (at least 10 baseline rows). It also demands creating bitmap indexes on the JOB_ID and DEPARTMENT_ID columns of the archive table, generating a materialized view named MV_DEPT_TURNOVER, and outputting an analysis report to /home/ga/Desktop/archive_analysis.txt. All names and constraints are strictly evaluated by the verifier scripts.
